author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
pradoṣa⋅samaye, śrīvāsa⋅aṅgane,
saṅgopane gorāmaṇi
śrī⋅harikīrtane, nāce nānā raṅge,
uṭhila maṅgaladhvani

mṛdaṅga⋅mādala, bāje karatāla,
mājhe mājhe jayatura
prabhura naṭana, dekhi’ sakalera,
hoila santāpa dūra

akhaṇḍa premete, mātala takhana,
sakala bhakatagaṇa
āpanā pāsari’, gorācā̐de gheri’,
nāce gāya anukṣaṇa

emata samaye, daiva⋅vyādhiyoge,
śrīvāsera antaḥpure
tanaya⋅viyoge, nārīgaṇa śoke,
prakāśala uccaiḥsvare

krandana uṭhile, ha’be rasabhaṅga,
bhakativinoda ḍare
śrīvāsa amani, bujhila kāraṇa,
paśila āpana ghare
en_translation:
At the time of twilight, in the courtyard of Śrīvāsa, Gaura⋅candra secretly dances with various ecstasies during the chanting of the Lord's names, and auspicious sounds arise.

Drums and cymbals sound, interspersed with calls of victory. Seeing the dancing of the Lord, everyone's distress was dispelled.

With unbroken love, all the devotees became intoxicated; forgetting themselves, they surrounded Gaura⋅candra, dancing and singing constantly.

At such a time, due to a divine illness, in the inner chambers of Śrīvāsa, the women cried out loudly in grief over the separation from a son.

Bhaktivinoda fears that if weeping arises, the spiritual mellow will be broken. Śrīvāsa immediately understood the reason and entered his own home.
